file_name: Benefit Breakdown Value for Both Parties.pdf
text: Benefit Breakdown – Value for Both Parties 1. Technical Support & Infrastructure The investor will provide technical guidance, system architecture support, cloud infrastructure advice, and development consultancy when required. In case of complex technical challenges, the investor’s team will assist in resolving them. 2. Strategic Mentorship & Advisory Role The investor will act as a strategic mentor and advisor, providing guidance on business growth, partnerships, scaling, and long-term vision. 3. Product & Market Readiness The investor will support in developing LimitX into a fully stable, secure, and market -ready product suitable for commercial launch and marketing. 4. Full-Time Commitment by Founders Both founders commit to working full -time on LimitX with complete dedication. All major development, operations, and growth efforts will be prioritized for this company. 5. Transparency & Access The founders will provide the investor access to: • Admin dashboard • Monthly performance analytics reports including key metrics (MRR, user growth, churn, engagement) • User and system reports to ensure transparency and trust. 6. Equity & Financial Rights The agreed deal is 2.5 million PKR for 10% equity. The investor will directly manage 50% of the marketing spend, which will not be given to the founders. Therefore, the founders will receive 1.25 million PKR as net investment. Equity ownership will be proportional to the net investment received and any future adjustments will be made accordingly. The investor will benefit from company growth, and future exit, acquisition, or merger according to their equity share. 7. Reporting & Communication The founders will provide regular progress updates through: • Weekly or bi-weekly reports, and/or • Online meetings covering completed work, upcoming plans, challenges, and milestones. 8. Workspace & Operational Support (Optional) If available, the investor may provide access to office space or on -site facilities to support the team’s productivity. 9. Decision-Making & Governance Major business decisions, including fundraising, acquisitions, and major partnerships, will be discussed mutually between founders and investor in good faith. 10. Term of Agreement Equity ownership remains unchanged; however, the mutual benefits contract will be valid for 1 year from the date of signing and may be renewed or revised upon mutual agreement. In the event of any future sale or transfer of this equity, I shall have the first right of refusal to purchase the equity on the same terms and conditions offered to any third party.

text: Investment Allocation Plan 1. Objective This document outlines the allocation of a total investment of PKR 1,200,000 for the development, deployment, and initial scaling of a cross-platform mobile application (iOS + Android), including infrastructure, hardware, AI integration, and human resources. 2. One-Time Capital Expenditures (CapEx) The following are essential upfront costs required to initiate development: • MacBook M1 (for iOS Development): PKR 275,000 • iPhone Device (for Testing & Deployment Validation): PKR 50,000 Total One-Time Cost: PKR 325,000 3. Recurring Operational Expenses (OpEx) 3.1 Infrastructure & Server Costs • Supabase (Primary Backend Database & Services): PKR 7,500 / month • Server 1 (Production Server – Groq APIs & Core Services): PKR 12,000 / month (estimated) • Server 2 (Backup & Redundancy Server): PKR 8,000 / month (estimated) Total Infrastructure Cost: PKR 27,500 / month 3.2 AI Model Development & Integration • Technology: On-device model training & integration • Monthly Cost VPS: $120 ≈ PKR 33,480 (Exchange Rate: 1 USD = 279 PKR) • Specs : 1 GPU RTX 4000 ; 32 GB RAM ; 8v CPU; 512GB Nvme • Implementation Duration: 3 months Total AI Development Cost (3 months): PKR 100,440 3.3 Human Resource Costs • UI/UX ; Devops : PKR 60,000 / month 3.4 Cross-Platform Development Scope • The product will be developed and maintained for both iOS and Android platforms, ensuring broader market reach. • Android development will run in parallel using shared backend infrastructure, minimizing additional fixed costs while increasing user acquisition potential. 4. Monthly Burn Rate Analysis Phase 1 (Months 1–3: Active AI Development + Full Infrastructure) • Infrastructure (Supabase + Servers): PKR 27,500 • Backend Engineer: PKR 60,000 • AI Development: PKR 33,480 Total Monthly Burn (Phase 1): PKR 120,980 Phase 2 (Post AI Integration – Month 4 Onwards) • Infrastructure (Supabase + Servers): PKR 27,500 • Backend Engineer: PKR 60,000 Total Monthly Burn (Phase 2): PKR 87,500 5. Total Investment Utilization Category Cost (PKR) One-Time Costs (Hardware) 325,000 AI Development (3 Months) 100,440 Total Initial Utilization 425,440 6. Runway Analysis Phase 1 (First 3 Months) • Monthly Burn: PKR 120,980 • Total for 3 Months: PKR 362,940 Total Spent After 3 Months: PKR 687,940 (including hardware) Remaining After 3 Months: PKR 512,060 Phase 2 Runway (Post Month 3) • Monthly Burn: PKR 87,500 Runway Duration: 512,060 ÷ 87,500 ≈ 5.85 months 7. Total Runway • Phase 1 Duration: 3 months • Phase 2 Duration: ~5.8 months Total Estimated Runway: ~8.8 to 9 months 8. Strategic Considerations • Remaining capital can be strategically allocated to: o Backup o Scaling infrastructure based on user growth

Business Development & Outreach Requirements 1. Email Setup Get 5 to 10 new email accounts specifically for sending cold emails. This keeps your main email safe from spam filters. 2. Portfolio & Profiles Website: Create a professional portfolio website to show our portfolios work. Social Media: Set up company pages and personal profiles on LinkedIn, Facebook, and Instagram. Freelance Platforms: Upwork and Fiverr accounts. 3. Project Videos (Loom) Record 3 to 5 videos using Loom. Speak in English to explain your best projects. Explain what service you provided and how the app actually works. 4. Automation & Dashboards Set up the automated system for sending pitches. Use the website you found to link everything together. Create a dashboard so you can see all your leads and progress in one place. • • • • • • • • • Strategic Outreach & Lead Generation Toolkit Here are the lists of tools within their pricing we work manually but don't reach the target and its very slow process so we need 1 lead information or email finding tool and 1 for mass sending emails. Tool Website Primary Function Cost / Access Level Instantly.ai instantly.ai Automated cold email outreach and domain warmup. Paid ($37/mo - Essential) Apollo.io apollo.io Lead sourcing; finding CTO/Founder emails by industry. 50 exports/month (Free) Hunter.io hunter.io Identifying email patterns for any specific company. 25 finds/month (Free) Apify apify.com Automated scraping of Google Maps for local leads. Free Tier available GMass gmass.co Sending mass cold email campaigns directly via Gmail. Free Tier available Streak CRM streak.com Email tracking and follow-up management within Gmail. 100% Free Calendly calendly.com Automated meeting booking link for proposals. 100% Free LinkedIn Ad Library linkedin.com/ad- library Finding businesses currently spending money on ads. 100% Free Recommended Selection for Efficiency: To solve the issue of slow manual processing and hit your targets, we recommend Apollo.io for lead information and Instantly.ai for mass sending to handle automation and warmup professionally.
